prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, composed to survive both a 16:10 article crop and a 16:9 card crop
Primary request: a clean editorial photograph about pairing a smart-home Matter device through a nearby Bluetooth proxy while the main home server is far away
Scene/backdrop: warm, lived-in residential living room with an oak console, linen lampshade, plaster wall, natural materials, and a softly blurred hallway or utility shelf in the distance
Subject: a homeowner's hand calmly placing a small unbranded green ESP32-style Bluetooth proxy board in a simple protective case beside an ordinary unbranded smart bulb and a small door sensor; a smartphone lies face-down and unused nearby; a compact home server is visible far in the background, softly out of focus
Style/medium: believable high-end residential editorial photography, not a product render or concept diagram
Composition/framing: landscape, eye-level three-quarter view, main hand/proxy/bulb grouping near the center so it remains clear at article-card size; uncluttered margins; no headline space needed
Lighting/mood: gentle late-afternoon window light, warm practical utility, calm and competent
Color palette: oak, cream, warm white, muted charcoal, restrained dark-green accent from the proxy board
Materials/textures: real wood grain, linen, matte plastic, lightly textured wall, natural skin texture
Constraints: no embedded text, no logos, no brand marks, no watermark; the proxy must look like a plausible small electronics board, not futuristic hardware; the phone screen must not be visible; only one person's hand, no faces needed
Avoid: headline poster, fake charts or graphs, routing/network/path lines, decorative connection overlays, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, holograms, neon cyber styling, generic AI diagram, piles of gadgets, impossible cables, distorted hands, duplicated objects
